helper_landscape_summary: Retrieved landscape shows two papers (Powerformer, ML4PS power-law biases) covering M_3 via explicit heavy-tail/power-law attention reweighting at func_score 0.60-0.65, and one paper (All-or-Here) covering M_4 via per-head routing at 0.45, but no single retrieved paper reaches the 0.7 threshold or jointly covers multiple sub-mechanisms.

hints.hint_text: Search whether Powerformer (2502.06151) or the ML4PS power-law-bias paper, under alternative terminology like 'Levy attention' or 'stable-distribution attention', also implements per-head routing between local heavy-tail and global decay (potentially covering M_3 + M_4 jointly).
Search for prior art on learned per-head tail-index (alpha) parameters in transformer attention.
Search for transformer papers that COMBINE heavy-tailed/power-law attention reweighting WITH per-head local-vs-global routing in a single architecture.
Search for survey/taxonomy papers on 'efficient attention' (ALiBi/RoPE/Kerple families) to identify the bucket a heavy-tailed-decay-plus-routing mechanism falls under.
Search arXiv listings late 2025 to May 2026 for unindexed preprints on alpha-stable / Levy-flight / heavy-tailed attention in LLMs.

solver_traces.reasoning: Powerformer applies fixed smooth heavy-tail decay to causal weights; does not include per-head routing or α-stable parametric fit. ML4PS paper similarly applies fixed power-law bias term per layer, no per-head split.

solver_traces.search_query: learned per-head tail-index alpha-stable attention transformer
solver_traces.reasoning: No standalone per-head learned tail-index publication surfaced. Consensus paper is orthogonal (training stabilization). M_1 candidate is plausibly novel as a standalone sub-mechanism.

solver_traces.search_query: transformer combine heavy-tail attention per-head routing single architecture
solver_traces.reasoning: All-or-Here demonstrates per-head global-vs-local routing but does NOT fit heavy-tail parameters per head. No paper combines heavy-tail fit with per-head split. Composition-level novelty plausible.

solver_traces.search_query: efficient attention taxonomy ALiBi RoPE Kerple heavy-tail bias survey
solver_traces.reasoning: Existing taxonomy buckets {scaled dot-product, ALiBi additive, RoPE rotary, Kerple kernelized, sparse top-k, sliding window, sink}. Heavy-tail-fit-and-renormalize would open a new bucket {parametric distribution renormalization}. Not in any existing taxonomy survey.
No directly-matching preprint found in May 2026. Contemporaneous-development risk exists in HT-SR adjacent line.

tree_stream_rationale: H1 reports 0.50 (Powerformer covers M_3 partially); H3 reports 0.55 (composition close to but not at Powerformer + All-or-Here joint level). max sim 0.55 < 0.7 threshold. H1 verdict not_anticipated but synthesizer notes step 10 mechanical FAIL drives final verdict via 5 surface-keyword hits. tree-stream is FAIL by synthesis but functional novelty is borderline (per-hint sims 0.35-0.55).
